diagrams.title: PRNG vs TRNG Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows the fundamental difference between pseudo-random and true random number generation approaches
elements: TRNG: Physical Source (noise/decay) → Entropy → Random Sequence
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the two fundamental approaches to random number generation and their characteristics

diagrams.title: Box-Muller Transform Flow
diagrams.description: Visualizes how two uniform random numbers are transformed into two normally distributed values
elements: Input: Two independent uniform U(0,1) values (U1, U2)
Output: Z0 = r×cos(θ), Z1 = r×sin(θ) - both standard normal
diagrams.purpose: Learning the transformation from uniform to Gaussian distribution for Monte Carlo and simulation applications

diagrams.title: LCG Algorithm Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the components and data flow of Linear Congruential Generator
elements: Seed X₀ → Multiply by 'a' (multiplier) → Add 'c' (increment) → Modulo 'm' (modulus) → Output X₁ → Feedback as next input
Cycle repeats when Xₙ = X₀ (period reached)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the simplest and most fundamental PRNG algorithm and its parameter selection impact

diagrams.title: CSPRNG Entropy Flow
diagrams.description: Illustrates how entropy is collected and used in cryptographically secure random generation
elements: Entropy Sources: Keyboard/mouse timing, disk I/O timing, hardware interrupts
Entropy Pool: Accumulates random bits from sources
CSPRNG Algorithm: SHA-256, ChaCha20 applied to entropy pool
Uses: Key generation, IV/nonce creation, session tokens
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how secure systems generate unpredictable random values for security-critical applications
